question_text: What is the amplitude of a wave?

options.label: A
options.text: The maximum displacement of a particle from its rest position

options.label: B
options.text: The distance the wave travels in one complete oscillation

options.label: C
options.text: The time taken for one complete wave to pass a point

options.label: D
options.text: The number of peaks in the wave per second

explanation: Amplitude is how far a particle moves from its resting position, either up or down. It determines the energy in the wave—larger amplitude means more energy. Option B describes wavelength, C describes period, and D describes frequency.
